CONTENT.Career: Cissoko joined NEC in 2016, signing his first professional contract with the club in January 2021. He made his Eredivisie debut in a 1–0 defeat to Utrecht on 29 April 2022, and scored his first league goal on 8 May 2022 in a 1–0 victory over Go Ahead Eagles. Cissoko signed a new contract with NEC in July 2022, linking him to the club until the summer of 2024, with the option of an extra season. He scored his first league goal of the 2022–23 season in a 1–1 draw against Groningen, an 88th-minute equaliser.In June 2023, Cissoko joined Ligue 1 club Toulouse. He sustained a foot injury in the summer, leaving him unable to make his competitive debut with Toulouse until 5 November 2023.On 9 July 2024, Cissoko joined EFL Championship club Plymouth Argyle on a season-long loan deal.
CONTENT.Personal life: Born in the Netherlands, Cissoko is of Guinean descent.
CONTENT.Style of play: Cissoko has been described as a quick winger who has the potential to form formidable and pacey partnerships up front.
CONTENT.External links: Ibrahim Cissoko at SoccerwayIbrahim Cissoko at ErediviseIbrahim Cissoko at FootballDatabase.eu
CONTENT.International career: In December 2023, Cissoko was pre-called up by the Guinea national football team for the 2023 Africa Cup of Nations. On March 21, 2024, he made his debut for Jong Oranje in a friendly match against Norway.

SUMMARY:
Ibrahim Cissoko (born 26 March 2003) is a Guinean-Dutch professional footballer who plays as a winger for EFL Championship side Plymouth Argyle, on loan from  Ligue 1 
club Toulouse.
